regression_risks: write_scope glob grammar: 'support/*' does not cover nested paths (support/checkers/, support/recording/, etc.); any Brick declaration using a single-level glob remains at risk of the same OOB rejection — observed in adapter-30-s1-park

node_modules write always-forbidden: any work step that runs npm/node install in a support/dashboard context will produce the same forbidden-path adapter-error regardless of write_scope; dashboard-productization 0612 and 0612b show this recurs without constraint in the task

Retry-as-new-building without root-cause repair: dashboard-productization-0612 → 0612b issued an identical retry 52 minutes later with no write_scope or task change; the retry pattern is itself a risk signal for any blocked building

Forbidden return key scanning: any agent that returns a nested dict with 'status', 'movement', or other reserved key anywhere in the return tree triggers adapter rejection; future closure bricks using complex return shapes must validate that no internal key matches the forbidden set

Agent writing to status/inbox: provider-ladder-fleet-presets work step attempted to emit a system-event to status/inbox — if future work steps internally invoke building-finished or notification mechanics, they risk the same forbidden-path error

Large-session codex timeout: F14 design step timed out on a bounded 5-file reading task (late-night provider degradation candidate per operator notes); any codex session with large context or late-night timing is at risk without a bounded timeout fallback
